name: Pork Diane Skillet Supper
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Pork tenderloins, cut cross-wise into 1/4-inch slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt margarine in a 12-inch skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add pork slices; cook 3 to 5 minutes or until browned on both sides. Remove pork from skillet; set aside.
2. Reserve 1/4 cup of the broth. Add remaining broth, Worcestershire sauce, salt, pepper, and potatoes to skillet. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low; cover and simmer 15 minutes or until potatoes are tender.
3. Stir in mushrooms, onions, and pork slices. Cover; simmer an additional 5 minutes or until pork is no longer pink in the center.
4. In small bowl, combine flour and reserved 1/4 cup broth; blend until smooth. Gradually stir into pork mixture. Cook and stir over medium-high heat until mixture is bubbly and thickened.
Nutrition Information Per Serving:
420 Calories, 11 g Fat, 620 mg Sodium
